$comment: PG&E agricultural rate card. REFERENCE DATA used to compute counterfactuals (what a meter WOULD cost on another rate), to recompute posted bills, and to MODEL spend for usage-only meters with no bill (src/lib/energy/modeled-cost.ts). The farmer's ACTUAL cost is always read from their own bills when present. Two TOU clocks, never conflated (src/lib/energy/tou.ts): the AG RATE peak is 5-8pm year-round daily (modern AG) or noon-6pm (legacy AG-4/AG-5); 4-9pm is the PDP/DR EVENT window only. MODERN AG (A1/A2/B/C) energy + demand are now the OFFICIAL bundled-total SECONDARY-voltage rates eff 2026-03-01 (Advice 7846-E), sourced from docs/batth-analysis/brief-pge-ag-rates.md (PG&E ELEC_SCHEDS_AG.pdf, cross-checked vs the 3CE rate sheet). KNOWN LIMITATION: HAGC/HAGA1/HAGA2/HAGB are AG taken at TRANSMISSION voltage and carry LOWER max-demand charges (AG-C transmission max-demand is 6.45/kW vs the 21.43 secondary used here), so this single-voltage card OVERSTATES max-demand for H-prefixed meters; the summer peak-period demand (29.92/kW) is voltage-independent and correct for all. LEGACY AG-4/AG-5 demand charges are official; their energy rates remain representative (PG&E publishes legacy energy as generation-only; total bundled awaits a summer bill). partial_peak only receives kWh on legacy three-tier rates (AG-4C/AG-5C); modern AG carries it equal to off_peak and receives 0 kWh there.
source: Modern AG (A1/A2/B/C) customer charge, demand charges, and TOU energy are the official bundled-total secondary-voltage rates eff 2026-03-01 per docs/batth-analysis/brief-pge-ag-rates.md (PG&E ELEC_SCHEDS_AG.pdf + 3CE rate sheet). AG-C summer peak-period demand corrected to 29.92/kW (was a placeholder 8-9); AG-A1 and AG-B demand charges removed (those schedules have none); AG-A2 max-demand corrected to 11.79 secondary. Legacy AG-4/AG-5 demand charges are official; legacy energy is representative pending a summer bill.

plans.sourceNote: OFFICIAL eff 2026-03-01 (ELEC_SCHEDS_AG, secondary, bundled): customer/day 0.68895, NO demand charge, summer peak 0.51620 / off 0.35026, winter peak 0.33989 / off 0.31060. AG-A1 recovers everything through energy (highest energy of the AG family, zero demand).

OFFICIAL eff 2026-03-01 (ELEC_SCHEDS_AG, secondary, bundled): customer/day 0.68895, max demand 11.79 (secondary; primary 13.95, transmission 4.68), NO peak-period demand, summer peak 0.40475 / off 0.23882, winter peak 0.24571 / off 0.21642.

OFFICIAL eff 2026-03-01 (ELEC_SCHEDS_AG, secondary, bundled): customer/day 0.91565, NO demand charge (energy-only recovery), summer peak 0.51289 / off 0.34004, winter peak 0.33597 / off 0.30671. PG&E does not tier AG-B by size; both tiers share these rates.
OFFICIAL eff 2026-03-01 (ELEC_SCHEDS_AG, secondary, bundled): same as AG-B1 (PG&E does not tier AG-B by size). NO demand charge.

OFFICIAL eff 2026-03-01 (ELEC_SCHEDS_AG, secondary, bundled): customer/day 1.43343, max demand 21.43 (secondary; primary 24.95, transmission 6.45), SUMMER peak-period demand 29.92/kW (5-8pm, all voltages), summer peak 0.21329 / off 0.17385, winter peak 0.18550 / off 0.15981, demand-charge limiter 0.50/kWh. NOTE: HAGC meters are AG-C at transmission voltage -> max demand is 6.45, not 21.43 (peak-period demand 29.92 is the same); this card uses the secondary value and overstates HAGC max-demand.

OFFICIAL eff 2026-03-01 (ELEC_SCHEDS_AG, secondary, bundled): same as AG-C1 (PG&E does not tier AG-C by size). Max demand 21.43 secondary, summer peak-period demand 29.92/kW, limiter 0.50/kWh. HAGC = AG-C at transmission voltage (max demand 6.45, overstated here).

AG-4C eff 2026-03-01 (ELEC_SCHEDS_AG-4, Sheet 6): customer/day 2.15003, max demand 16.22 su/wi, summer peak-period demand 7.08, energy peak-su 0.23620 / part-su 0.23567 / off-su 0.22387 / part-wi 0.21302 / off-wi 0.21231 (total bundled). Legacy summer = May-Oct, peak noon-6pm. Closed schedule.
AG-4C eff 2026-03-01 (ELEC_SCHEDS_AG-4, Sheet 6): same as the small tier (Batth's legacy AG-4 meters are AG-4C). Max demand 16.22, summer peak-period demand 7.08.

AG-5C eff 2026-03-01 (ELEC_SCHEDS_AG-5, Sheet 6): customer/day 5.30871, max demand 14.90 su/wi (OFFICIAL), summer peak-period demand 14.57 (OFFICIAL, was placeholder 9). Energy remains representative (PG&E publishes legacy energy as generation-only; total bundled awaits a summer bill). Legacy summer = May-Oct, peak noon-6pm.

AG-5B eff 2026-03-01 (ELEC_SCHEDS_AG-5, Sheet 6): customer/day 1.19446, max demand 27.89 summer / 20.54 winter (OFFICIAL; was 20.54 both), summer peak-period demand 10.45 (OFFICIAL, was placeholder 10). Energy remains representative pending a summer bill.
